title: Melted Buttery Uruguayan Tortilla Onigiri
description: A romantic Uruguayan dish featuring flour tortilla, nigiri-shaping, and a buttery glassy-jelly texture, served on a cedar plank.
instructions: In a small saucepan, melt the butter over low heat.
Add the water, salt, and pepper to the saucepan and stir until the salt is dissolved.
Cut the flour tortillas into triangles or use a cookie cutter to create shapes.
Place a small amount of the melted butter mixture onto the center of each tortilla triangle.
Fold the tortilla triangles into nigiri shapes and press gently to seal.
Place the shaped tortillas onto the soaked cedar planks.
Drizzle the remaining butter mixture over the tortillas.
Sprinkle the chopped parsley over the tortillas.
Drizzle the lemon juice over the parsley.
Bake the tortillas in the preheated oven for 10 minutes or until they are lightly browned and the butter is melted and glassy.
